description:
**Problem**
Graph/metadata command families are still split across legacy `beads-rs` handlers (`comments`, `dep`, `label`, `epic`, `deleted`, `subscribe`), which blocks a clean ownership boundary for CLI behavior.

**Design**
Port these command handlers into `crates/beads-cli/src/commands/` in one tranche focused on behavior parity and shared render/helper reuse. Leave explicit shims in `beads-rs` until the full epic closes.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `comments`, `dep`, `label`, `epic`, `deleted`, and `subscribe` handlers are owned by `beads-cli`.
- [ ] Legacy handlers in `beads-rs` are reduced to call-through shims.
- [ ] Subcommand name mapping and aliases remain stable.
- [ ] Regression tests cover dep tree/cycles, comment add/list, label add/rm/list, epic status, and deleted/subscription flows.
- [ ] Full verification gate passes (`fmt`, `dylint`, `clippy`, `test`, `slow-tests`).

**Files**
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/comments.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/dep.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/label.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/epic.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/deleted.rs
- crates/beads-cli/src/commands/subscribe.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/{comments,dep,label,epic,deleted,subscribe}.rs
title: Migrate comments/dep/label/epic/deleted/subscribe handlers to beads-cli

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Previous checkpoint payload is validated before reuse in export path.
- [ ] Invalid previous payload falls back to full export automatically.
- [ ] Invalid checkpoint payload is not persisted in cache/memory as reusable state.
- [ ] Tests cover invalid-previous fallback and no repeated publish-fail loop.
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint export/publish can reuse previous checkpoint payload before strict validation, which can create repeated publish failures instead of self-healing full rebuild.

**Files**: crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/git_worker.rs, crates/beads-git/src/checkpoint/publish.rs
design:
**Design**
Harden previous-payload reuse:
- validate `previous` payload with strict import before reuse
- if invalid, drop `previous` and export full (`previous: None`)
- avoid caching in-memory invalid export payloads before successful publish

Ensure publish path converges to a healthy checkpoint without manual intervention.
title: Migration slice 7: Harden checkpoint previous-payload reuse

description: Forward sequence gaps can advance max/next while contiguous watermark lags. Replace implicit gap tolerance with explicit gap error state; disallow committing next-origin-seq from non-contiguous state.
title: P0: Forbid non-contiguous replay progression from committing allocator next-seq

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Internal repl message types cannot be constructed without valid head/seq combinations.
- [ ] Decode rejects `seq>0` without head and `seq=0` with head (clear error).
- [ ] Encoding preserves canonical ordering and wire compatibility.
- [ ] Tests cover valid/invalid ACKs and HELLO/WELCOME watermark snapshots.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.
description:
**Problem**
Replication wire structs (`Ack`, `Hello`, `Welcome`) carry `WatermarkMap` plus optional `WatermarkHeads`. There is no type-level coupling between `seq` and `head`, so it is easy to construct invalid states (e.g., `seq>0` with no head). The compiler cannot prevent this; errors only surface later as runtime failures (`MissingHead` / divergence), and these failures are not localized to the parsing boundary.

This violates parse-don’t-validate and makes correctness depend on call-site discipline.

**Impact**
- Incorrect or missing heads can stall replication or mark peers diverged.
- Internal code can accidentally emit invalid ACKs without compile-time feedback.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/proto.rs` (Ack/Hello/Welcome decode)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (watermark snapshot/seed)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs`
design:
**Design**
Introduce a typed, invariant-preserving watermark representation at the Repl boundary:

- Create an internal type that represents *validated* watermarks (use `Watermarks<K>` or a new `WatermarkSnapshot<K>` that contains `Watermark<K>` values).
- Keep wire format unchanged by introducing wire-only structs:
  - `WireAck` / `WireHello` / `WireWelcome` with `WatermarkMap` + `WatermarkHeads` (serde/cbor only)
  - `Ack` / `Hello` / `Welcome` used internally with `Watermarks<K>` (or validated snapshot type)
- `decode_*` should parse into wire structs, then immediately validate/convert into the typed representation:
  - Missing head for seq>0 becomes a `ProtoDecodeError::InvalidField`.
  - Genesis (`seq=0`) must have no head (or Genesis marker), enforced by `Watermark::new`.
- `encode_*` should accept the typed representation and derive wire maps/heads in canonical order.

**Ordering / invariants preserved**
- Maintain deterministic ordering by using `BTreeMap` during wire conversion.
- No change to wire encoding order or shape; only validation is moved to the parse boundary.
title: Repl watermark heads must be coupled to seq at parse boundary
